message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Re-review: blocking items fixed, CI green">
Luca has addressed all blocking review items and CI is green. Reviewers who had blocking items — please re-review to verify your concerns are resolved:

- Steve Krug, Lea Verou, Steve Schoger: context headings/grouping
- Carson Gross: filter partial swap
- Heydon Pickering: focus management + visible label on context select
- Marty Cagan: your 2 blocking items

If satisfied, please send your consensus approval to luca-palmieri. Kent Beck and Scott Wlaschin already approved.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me re-review the updated code to verify my blocking items are resolved.
The blocking issue is fully resolved. Let me send my consensus approval.

input.content:
## Re-review: BLOCKING issue resolved -- Consensus APPROVED

I've verified the updated code. My blocking item is fully addressed:

**Context grouping in "All" view** -- Confirmed fixed. The template now correctly branches:
- `selected_context.is_none()` (All view): renders `<section>` elements with `aria-labelledby` pointing to `<h2>` context headings, each wrapping their own `<ul>` -- exactly the structure I recommended.
- Single context filter: renders a flat `<ul>` (correct, since it is already scoped to one context).

**CSS** -- `.next-actions__group` and `.next-actions__context-heading` (lines 1510-1519) match my suggested styles: `--font-size-lg`, `--font-semibold`, `--color-fg-muted` for the heading, `--space-lg` margin on the group. Visual hierarchy is clear: h1 "Next Actions" > h2 "@computer" > item titles.

**My 2 Minor items remain** (redundant select styles, missing forced-colors on delete button), but these are non-blocking and can be addressed in a future cleanup pass.

CSS/Frontend review: **Approved.** The implementation uses tokens consistently, logical properties throughout, proper BEM naming, correct cascade layer placement, mobile-first approach, and the context grouping provides the visual scanning structure required by US-GTD-3.
input.summary: Re-review approved: blocking item fixed, consensus given

routing.content:
## Re-review: BLOCKING issue resolved -- Consensus APPROVED

I've verified the updated code. My blocking item is fully addressed:

**Context grouping in "All" view** -- Confirmed fixed. The template now correctly branches:
- `selected_context.is_none()` (All view): renders `<section>` elements with `aria-labelledby` pointing to `<h2>` context headings, each wrapping their own `<ul>` -- exactly the structure I recommended.
- Single context filter: renders a flat `<ul>` (correct, since it is already scoped to one context).

**CSS** -- `.next-actions__group` and `.next-actions__context-heading` (lines 1510-1519) match my suggested styles: `--font-size-lg`, `--font-semibold`, `--color-fg-muted` for the heading, `--space-lg` margin on the group. Visual hierarchy is clear: h1 "Next Actions" > h2 "@computer" > item titles.

content.text:
Blocking issue verified as resolved. I've sent my **consensus approval** to the Driver. 

Summary of re-review:
- The "All" view now renders context groups with `<section>` + `aria-labelledby` + `<h2>` headings (lines 67-77 of `next_actions.html`)
- The CSS for `.next-actions__group` and `.next-actions__context-heading` (lines 1510-1519 of `main.css`) matches my recommended styles exactly
- Single-context filtered view correctly renders a flat list
- Visual hierarchy is clear: h1 > h2 context headings > item titles
- Two Minor items (redundant select styles, forced-colors on delete) noted as non-blocking for future cleanup
